verdict_reason: The engineering layer preserves the three-phase Sanskrit compound structure (avyakta-ādīni / vyakta-madhyāni / avyakta-nidhanāni) without flattening it. Shankara's compositional reading (beings as composite) and Ramanuja's epistemic reading (stages unknown vs. known) are both honestly summarised in the source pack and not conflated in the engineering layer; the layer adopts the structural reading both share without claiming either tradition exclusively. The rhetorical 'tatra kā paridevanā' is preserved as 'What lamentation is there in this?' Technical terms (paridevanā, avyakta) are not dropped but rendered through the three-phase metaphor.
